rs1801133.description: MTHFR is the key enzyme turning folate into methylfolate for DNA repair, neurotransmitters and detox.

rs2236225.description: MTHFD1 supports folate cycling and methylation robustness.

rs1805087.description: MTR uses B12 to remethylate homocysteine, affecting methylation and cardiovascular health.

rs1801394.description: MTRR regenerates methylated B12; affects homocysteine and methylation stability.

rs762551.description: CYP1A2 controls caffeine clearance rate and cardiovascular response.

rs662.description: PON1 breaks down oxidized LDL and supports antioxidant defenses.

rs1695.description: GSTP1 attaches glutathione to toxins for removal from cells.

rs1051740.description: EPHX1 detoxifies reactive epoxides from smoke, pollutants and drugs.

rs1800566.description: NQO1 clears quinones and supports antioxidant recycling.

rs5082.description: APOA2 influences response to saturated fat and weight gain.

rs328.description: LPL breaks down circulating triglycerides after meals.

rs662799.description: APOA5 strongly influences fasting and post-meal triglycerides.

rs1800588.description: LIPC regulates HDL production and liver lipid processing.

rs9939609.description: FTO influences hunger, satiety and obesity risk.

variants.TT: Lower appetite / lower weight gain risk

rs6232.description: PCSK1 activates satiety hormones; weaker variants reduce fullness signaling.

rs17782313.description: MC4R affects hunger drive and energy balance.

variants.CC: Higher appetite drive / weight gain tendency

rs7903146.description: TCF7L2 is one of the strongest common genetic factors for type 2 diabetes risk.

variants.TT: Higher T2D risk / fragile glucose handling

rs1260326.description: GCKR influences fasting glucose and triglycerides via liver glucose regulation.

rs13266634.description: SLC30A8 affects zinc transport in beta cells, influencing insulin release.

rs10830963.description: MTNR1B affects insulin secretion timing and fasting glucose levels.

rs4880.description: SOD2 neutralizes superoxide radicals in mitochondria.

rs10741657.description: CYP2R1 converts vitamin D from skin/food into 25(OH)D, the main circulating form.

rs7041.description: GC (DBP) binds and transports vitamin D in the blood.

rs602662.description: FUT2 affects B12 absorption and secretor status, shaping gut microbiome composition.

rs1801198.description: TCN2 transports B12 to cells; variants influence B12 needs.

rs11645428.description: BCMO1 converts plant carotenoids to active vitamin A.

rs1799945.description: HFE regulates iron uptake. Some variants increase risk of iron overload.

rs4680.description: COMT regulates breakdown of dopamine and adrenaline.

rs6265.description: BDNF supports neuron growth, synaptic plasticity and resilience to stress.

rs1800497.description: This variant affects dopamine receptor levels and reward sensitivity.

rs1042713.description: ADRB2 responds to adrenaline; affects stress response and exercise performance.

rs1042714.description: Affects how quickly beta-2 receptors downregulate under chronic stress/stimulants.

rs2010963.description: VEGFA regulates new blood vessel formation and endurance adaptations.

rs1815739.description: ACTN3 influences sprint/power ability vs endurance specialization.

rs17602729.description: AMPD1 recycles AMP during high-intensity effort. Some alleles cause quicker fatigue.

rs12722.description: COL5A1 influences collagen structure affecting flexibility vs stiffness.

rs1800592.description: UCP1 uncouples mitochondria in brown fat to generate heat instead of ATP.

rs659366.description: UCP2 modulates mitochondrial coupling and baseline metabolism.

rs57875989.description: PER3 influences morningness vs eveningness, sleep depth and circadian stability.

rs1801260.description: CLOCK variants affect sleep duration, metabolic timing and circadian rhythm stability.

rs2304672.description: NR1D1 regulates sleep pressure and metabolic circadian signaling.

rs1800629.description: TNF-alpha is a master inflammatory cytokine influencing CRP and immune response.

rs1800795.description: IL6 influences CRP levels, inflammation, overtraining sensitivity and stress response.

rs2476601.description: PTPN22 variants alter immune tolerance and T-cell activation thresholds.

rs3024493.description: IL10 controls resolution of inflammation. Lower activity = prolonged inflammatory response.

rs429358.description: One of two key SNPs defining APOE2/3/4 isoforms impacting lipids and brain aging.

rs7412.description: Together with rs429358 determines APOE2, APOE3 or APOE4.

rs699.description: AGT influences renin–angiotensin activity and salt sensitivity.

rs1799752.description: The ACE I/D polymorphism influences vascular tone and endurance performance.

variants.DD: Higher ACE activity / higher BP risk

rs5186.description: AGTR1 modulates vascular constriction and BP reactivity.

rs2235544.description: DIO1 converts T4 into active T3, affecting metabolic rate and energy.

rs225014.description: DIO2 activates T3 locally in the brain, muscles, and fat tissue.

rs12594956.description: PGC-1α controls mitochondrial creation and endurance adaptation.

rs699005.description: COL1A1 influences collagen type I structure; important for tendon stability.

rs9340799.description: ESR1 variants influence estrogen signaling affecting metabolism, mood, and muscle.

rs9282858.description: SRD5A2 converts testosterone into DHT; affects sex drive, aggression, and hair.

rs2071749.description: HMOX1 promotes breakdown of heme and protects against oxidative stress.

rs1056836.description: CYP1B1 detoxifies polycyclic aromatics and metabolizes steroid hormones.

rs1048943.description: CYP1A1 detoxifies smoke compounds, pollutants, and dioxins.

rs2740574.description: CYP3A4 metabolizes ~50% of all medications and many toxins.

rs1799983.description: Determines vasodilation capacity, BP regulation, and exercise oxygen delivery.

rs4343.description: Associated with vascular tone and aerobic capacity.

rs5516.description: The major common variant for increased clot risk.

rs6269.description: Regulates COMT enzyme production affecting dopamine tone.

rs3813034.description: Influences stress sensitivity, mood stability and SSRI response.

rs350843.description: FOXO3 is one of the strongest longevity-associated genes.

rs2802292.description: Another FOXO3 site associated with survival under stress.

rs1800498.description: Influences food reward sensitivity and overeating risk.

rs987237.description: Associated with appetite strength and emotional eating.

rs1801131.description: MTHFR A1298C influences methylation cofactor balance and neurotransmitter synthesis.

variants.CA: Moderately reduced flexibility in methylation cycle
variants.AC: Moderately reduced flexibility in methylation cycle

rs3733890.description: BHMT provides a backup route to remethylate homocysteine using betaine.

rs234706.description: CBS diverts homocysteine into sulfur detox and glutathione production.

variants.GA: Shifted flux (may lower homocysteine, increase sulfur load)
variants.AG: Shifted flux (may lower homocysteine, increase sulfur load)

rs1979277.description: SHMT1 helps partition folate between DNA synthesis and methylation reactions.

rs5882.description: CETP transfers cholesterol esters between HDL and other lipoproteins.

variants.AG: Slightly reduced CETP activity (often higher HDL)
variants.GA: Slightly reduced CETP activity (often higher HDL)
variants.GG: Lower CETP activity (HDL may be higher, but particle quality varies)

rs1801282.description: PPARG influences insulin sensitivity and whether fat is stored more subcutaneously or viscerally.

variants.CG: Slightly improved insulin sensitivity in some contexts
variants.GC: Slightly improved insulin sensitivity in some contexts

rs1501299.description: ADIPOQ affects adiponectin, a hormone that improves insulin sensitivity and fat burning.

rs1137101.description: LEPR variants influence how strongly the brain responds to leptin (satiety hormone).

rs1799883.description: FABP2 influences how efficiently dietary fat is absorbed in the small intestine.

rs1205.description: CRP is a major acute-phase protein; this SNP affects typical baseline CRP levels.

rs16944.description: IL1B regulates acute inflammation. Strong alleles increase injury sensitivity.

rs2228145.description: IL6R modulates IL-6 signaling; some variants shift soluble vs membrane-bound receptor balance.

rs731236.description: VDR mediates vitamin D effects on immunity, bone and metabolism.

rs1544410.description: Another common VDR site shaping tissue sensitivity to vitamin D.

rs6311.description: HTR2A affects cortical serotonin signaling, stress processing and sleep architecture.

rs6295.description: HTR1A autoreceptors regulate serotonin release and feedback inhibition.

rs3731249.description: CDKN2A regulates cell cycle arrest and protects against uncontrolled cell growth.

rs25487.description: XRCC1 repairs single-strand DNA breaks caused by oxidation and pollutants.

rs13181.description: ERCC2 repairs bulky DNA adducts from smoke, pollutants and oxidative stress.

rs1801516.description: ATM detects and repairs DNA double-strand breaks and regulates apoptosis.

rs11615.description: ERCC1 participates in excision repair of damaged DNA bases.

rs1042522.description: TP53 regulates apoptosis and DNA repair; key in preventing damaged cell survival.

rs1799950.description: BRCA1 helps repair double-strand DNA breaks with high fidelity.

rs16942.description: Another BRCA1 site influencing repair capacity and cell stability.

rs601338.description: Determines whether ABO antigens appear in gut mucosa; major driver of microbiome composition.

rs4988235.description: Controls expression of lactase enzyme in adulthood.

rs11185098.description: DAO breaks down histamine in the gut; variants affect intolerance risk.

variants.AA: Low DAO activity (higher histamine intolerance risk)

rs2052129.description: HDC produces histamine; variants influence histamine load.

rs17616434.description: ALDH2 detoxifies acetaldehyde; impaired variants increase inflammation and gut sensitivity.

rs1229984.description: ADH1B converts ethanol to acetaldehyde; fast variants cause intense flushing.

rs17653216.description: MGAM helps break down starches; variants affect carb tolerance.

rs10065172.description: IRGM regulates autophagy in intestinal cells; variants affect IBD risk.

rs2241880.description: NOD2 recognizes bacterial fragments; major IBD/Crohn’s gene.

rs17226398.description: Organic cation transporter for carnitine; affects metabolism & gut energy.

rs660339.description: Affects proton leak across mitochondrial membrane, altering ATP efficiency vs heat production.

variants.TT: Higher uncoupling (higher caloric burn, lower efficiency)

rs8192678.description: Master regulator of mitochondrial creation. Strong predictor of endurance and anti-fatigue.

rs2239681.description: Impacts efficiency of Complex I, influencing ATP output and fatigue tolerance.

rs8101881.description: Controls mtDNA copy number. Higher expression = greater mitochondrial population per cell.

rs1042173.description: Serotonin availability affects central fatigue and endurance.

rs4994.description: ADRB3 influences brown fat activation and baseline metabolic burn.

rs8192675.trait: GLUT4 glucose uptake in muscle cells
rs8192675.description: Determines how efficiently muscles pull glucose into mitochondria for ATP.

rs2070744.description: eNOS-derived nitric oxide regulates mitochondrial biogenesis and ATP effectiveness.

rs1800797.description: IL-6 drives mitochondrial repair post-exercise; altered variants slow recovery.

rs1049434.description: COL12A1 influences muscular–tendon integration and strain resilience.

rs2285477.description: Affects final electron transport step; alters peak aerobic capacity.

rs3741942.description: Critical for electron transfer through Complex I; low-function variants increase oxidative stress.

rs6259.description: SHBG binds testosterone and estrogen, controlling how much is free (active) in the body.

rs2486758.description: CYP17A1 regulates sex steroid production (DHEA → testosterone).

rs2293275.description: LHCGR controls how strongly testes/ovaries respond to LH to produce testosterone/estrogen.

rs6165.description: FSHR influences ovarian follicle maturation and Sertoli cell responsiveness.

rs6166.description: Affects ovarian stimulation response and testicular FSH responsiveness.

rs1047303.description: HSD17B3 is key for producing testosterone in testes/ovaries.

rs605059.description: ESR2 modulates estrogen response in brain, reproductive organs, and bone.

rs523349.description: Controls conversion of testosterone into DHT in skin, liver and brain.

rs3123554.description: Affects how tightly SHBG binds testosterone and estradiol.

rs9282859.description: HSD17B12 controls conversion of estrone to estradiol.

rs45446698.description: CYP3A5 accelerates metabolism of testosterone and cortisol.

rs1256049.description: A separate ESR1 site affecting estrogen sensitivity in metabolism and bone.

rs1800012.description: A major collagen variant affecting tendon stiffness, ligament stability, and bone strength.

variants.TT: Higher stiffness and lower hypermobility risk

rs1800255.trait: Collagen type III elasticity (skin, vessels, ligaments)
rs1800255.description: COL3A1 affects elasticity; weaker variants increase joint laxity and injury risk.

variants.TT: Higher elasticity and slightly more hypermobility

rs1800252.trait: Cartilage durability and joint surface quality
rs1800252.description: COL2A1 is essential for cartilage structure; variants shift knee/hip injury sensitivity.

rs2073711.description: Influences collagen fibril formation affecting joints, intervertebral discs, and ligaments.

rs2289360.description: Affects microfibrils in skin and ligaments and shifts tissue elasticity.

variants.TT: Higher elasticity and more joint laxity

rs137852.description: COL5A2 affects tendon stiffness and propensity for joint hypermobility.

rs679620.description: MMP3 remodels collagen; variants affect tendon healing speed and injury recurrence.

rs1800796.trait: IL-6 tendon healing and collagen synthesis
rs1800796.description: IL6 regulates collagen deposition during repair; low-activity variants heal more slowly.

rs143383.trait: Joint surface resilience and knee injury risk
rs143383.description: GDF5 influences cartilage formation, joint shape, and knee stability.

rs8031440.description: Aggrecan is the core cartilage proteoglycan; variants affect spine and knee resilience.

variants.TT: Lower aggrecan and higher mechanical wear

rs479744.description: PLOD1 stabilizes collagen fibers; weaker variants increase ligament laxity.

rs2271683.description: ELN controls elastin production, shaping flexibility vs stiffness in ligaments and skin.

rs1728986.description: TNXB regulates connective tissue integrity; risk variants show hypermobility tendency.

variants.TT: Higher joint laxity and hypermobility tendency

rs1800790.description: FGB affects micro-tear recovery and collagen deposition in tendons and ligaments.

rs5751876.description: ADORA2A dictates how strongly caffeine blocks adenosine, affecting anxiety, jitters, and insomnia.

rs893703.description: CYP2D6 metabolizes many stimulants, antidepressants, beta-blockers, and pain medications.

rs1042713_dupfix.description: This secondary ADRB2 SNP affects how easily fat stores are mobilized during exercise.

rs4880_dupfix.description: An additional SOD2-linked site affecting mitochondrial oxidative stress tolerance.

rs934078.description: Influences local tissue activation of thyroid hormone and metabolic speed.

rs2071403.description: THRB affects how tissues respond to circulating thyroid hormones T3 and T4.

rs738409.description: Strong predictor of fatty liver susceptibility depending on diet and alcohol intake.

variants.GG: High risk / low liver fat clearance

rs2740574_dupfix.description: This additional site affects steroid hormone and toxin clearance.

rs1815739_extra.description: A secondary ACTN3-linked site refining power vs endurance profile.

rs2854464.description: Secondary ACTN3 site influencing explosive strength.

rs13278062.description: SIRT1 regulates autophagy, DNA repair, metabolic aging, and protects against inflammation.

rs3758391.description: SIRT3 regulates antioxidant enzymes and mitochondrial longevity, reducing ROS damage.

rs3025039.description: VEGF influences microvascular integrity, nutrient delivery, and resilience to aging.

variants.TT: Reduced maintenance and slightly faster aging

rs2802295.description: FOXO1 regulates oxidative stress resistance, fasting response, and glucose aging pathways.

rs4654748.description: LMNA influences nuclear structural aging. Weak variants increase cellular senescence tendency.

rs17609940.description: IGF1R variants influence the growth–longevity tradeoff. Lower activity is typically longevity-favored.

rs2535633.description: ELOVL2 methylation is one of the strongest DNA methylation markers of biological age.

rs12203592.description: IRF4 affects pigmentation, UV sensitivity, and wrinkle formation rate.

rs1805007.description: MC1R is a major UV sensitivity gene; variants increase burning and collagen breakdown.

rs885479.description: Affects eumelanin/pheomelanin balance; higher pheomelanin increases oxidative stress.

rs2070974.description: TYR regulates melanin production. Low-function variants increase UV aging rate.

rs1042602.description: Common pigmentation SNP influencing sunburn risk and photoaging.

rs1110400.description: SOD1 neutralizes ROS generated by UV radiation and environmental stress.

rs3774175.description: SOD3 protects extracellular collagen from UV-induced radicals.

rs676925.description: MMP1 breaks down type I collagen. High-activity variants accelerate wrinkle formation.

rs559870.description: Influences collagen turnover; affects firmness vs sagging.

rs3737965.description: Affects basement membrane strength and long-term skin elasticity.

rs174537.description: Major regulator of EPA/DHA conversion and inflammation balance.

rs662799_TIER2.description: Strong triglyceride regulator affecting fasting & post-meal levels.

rs328_TIER2.description: Affects how fast blood lipids clear after meals.

rs10757278.description: Major non-lipid CAD variant affecting vascular remodeling.

rs11191548_TIER2.trait: Renal sodium handling via dopamine system
rs11191548_TIER2.description: Dopamine receptor variant influencing salt-sensitive BP regulation.

rs1800790_TIER2.description: Influences blood clot thickening and micro-damage recovery.

rs1254337.description: Major determinant of uric acid and metabolic inflammation tendencies.

rs558972.description: Glucokinase sets the glucose threshold for insulin release.

rs5219.description: Influences pancreatic channel gating and insulin secretion strength.

rs9923231.description: Affects clotting factor activation and vascular calcification resistance.

rs2228570.trait: Vitamin D receptor activity & muscle strength
rs2228570.description: VDR influences muscle fiber size, strength, and recovery capacity.

variants.CC: High muscle response to vitamin D

rs1805065.description: ACTN2 maintains Z-disc stability, supporting power output.

rs16835198.description: FNDC5 influences fatigue resistance and mitochondrial health.

rs4253778.description: PPARα determines fat vs glucose preference during long efforts.

rs1805086.description: MYF5 is a major regulator of new muscle fiber formation.

rs8111989.description: Secondary VDR site linked to fast-twitch recruitment and repair.

rs1049434_TIER2.description: Determines explosive power & fatigue resistance during repeated efforts.

rs53576.description: OXTR modulates the 'love hormone' oxytocin. Influences social bonding, empathy, and stress resilience.

variants.AA: Lower oxytocin sensitivity (More analytical / higher stress response)

rs324420.description: FAAH breaks down anandamide (the 'bliss molecule'). Variants affect pain tolerance and anxiety levels.

variants.AA: Slowest breakdown (High pain tolerance / Low anxiety)

rs33972313.description: Controls the transport of Vitamin C into the bloodstream. Critical for collagen and immunity.

rs7946.description: PEMT produces choline endogenously. Critical for liver health, especially in vegans/vegetarians.

variants.TT: Low synthesis (High risk of deficiency / liver fat)

rs6536.description: TRPM6 regulates magnesium levels in the kidneys and gut.

rs713598.description: Determines if you are a 'supertaster'. Affects vegetable intake (broccoli, coffee, dark chocolate).

rs12255372.description: A strong secondary marker for glucose handling and diabetes risk.

rs6323.description: MAOA breaks down serotonin and dopamine. The 'Warrior' variant breaks them down slowly, leading to mood swings and higher aggression under stress.

variants.T: Low activity (Warrior) - Higher risk of aggression/mood swings
variants.TT: Low activity (Warrior) - Higher risk of aggression/mood swings

rs17822931.description: Determines earwax type (wet vs dry) and body odor. Wet earwax is linked to stronger body odor.

variants.TT: Dry earwax / No body odor (East Asian trait)

rs5400.description: GLUT2 glucose sensor in the brain. Risk variants cause the brain to not sense sugar efficiently, leading to higher sugar consumption.

variants.TT: High sweet tooth / higher sugar intake

rs1799971.description: Influences pain perception and social rejection sensitivity. Risk variants require higher doses of pain medication.

variants.AG: Higher pain sensitivity / lower opiate response
variants.GA: Higher pain sensitivity / lower opiate response
variants.GG: Highest pain sensitivity / social distress sensitivity

rs2395185.description: A primary marker for HLA-DQ2.5. Presence increases risk of Celiac disease significantly (though does not guarantee it).

rs3749034.description: GAD1 converts glutamate (excitatory) into GABA (calming). Poor conversion is linked to anxiety and neuroticism.

variants.AA: Lower GABA levels / higher anxiety tendency

rs1049353.description: Affects the endocannabinoid system, influencing gut motility and stress response.

variants.TT: Associated with higher anxiety / abdominal obesity

rs4961.description: Alpha-adducin affects how the kidneys handle sodium. Risk variants lead to higher blood pressure when eating salt.

rs1800955.description: DRD4 regulates dopamine levels in the brain's reward centers. The 'T' variant is associated with 'Novelty Seeking' behavior and impulsivity.

variants.CC: Lower novelty seeking / higher impulse control

rs4149056.description: SLCO1B1 transports statins into the liver. Poor function (C allele) leads to high blood levels of statins, increasing the risk of muscle damage (myopathy).

variants.CC: Low transport (High risk of statin-induced muscle pain)

rs16969968.description: Influences the brain's response to nicotine. The 'A' variant is strongly linked to heavy smoking and difficulty quitting.

rs838133.description: FGF21 regulates simple sugar intake preference via the liver-brain axis. 'A' allele carriers tend to eat more candy and sweets.

rs324981.description: NPSR1 affects sleep duration and wakefulness. The 'T' allele is associated with needing significantly less sleep to function optimally.

variants.TT: Short sleeper trait (Function well on <6.5 hours)

rs1801253.description: ADRB1 controls beta-adrenergic receptors in the heart. It dictates how much your heart rate increases during exercise and response to beta-blockers.

variants.GG: Reduced response (Lower max HR / potential heart failure risk)

rs10993994.description: Associated with 'dispositional optimism' and general resilience to stress. Also protects against oxidative damage.

rs713041.description: GPX4 protects cell membranes (lipids) from oxidative death (ferroptosis). Critical for brain and vessel health.

rs6020.description: A major risk factor for venous thrombosis (blood clots). The 'A' variant is the Prothrombin G20210A mutation.

rs1333049.description: The most powerful genetic marker for non-familial coronary artery disease (CAD) risk.

rs174547.description: FADS2 is critical for converting plant-based Omega-3 (ALA) into functional DHA and EPA. The 'C' allele is less efficient.

variants.CC: Low conversion efficiency (High requirement for pre-formed DHA/EPA)

rs1010151.description: GLO1 detoxifies precursors of AGEs (products of sugar binding to proteins). The 'A' variant reduces detoxification efficiency, accelerating aging effects.

rs3827760.description: This variant affects hair shaft thickness and shape of incisor teeth (shovel shape). Common in East Asian populations.

rs17608.description: Common variant leading to partial deficiency of AAT, which protects the lungs from damage, increasing risk of COPD and liver disease.

rs12942023.description: Involved in the detoxification of congeners and toxins often found in aged/dark alcohols (whiskey, wine). 'G' allele is less efficient.

variants.GG: Reduced congener tolerance (Higher hangover risk from dark liquors)

rs1800562.description: The primary risk factor for Hereditary Hemochromatosis (severe iron overload). This variant (C282Y) is much more impactful than the H63D variant found in most panels.

rs12785878.description: DHCR7 turns cholesterol in the skin into the precursor for Vitamin D. Risk variants produce less Vitamin D from sunlight exposure.

variants.TT: Low production from sun (Higher dietary need)

rs1256335.description: ALPL regulates the breakdown of B6 into a form cells can uptake. Risk variants are linked to lower cellular B6 levels despite normal intake.

variants.CC: Lower B6 levels (May need active P5P form)

rs2231142.description: A critical transporter for flushing uric acid out of the body. Poor function leads to urate crystal buildup and gout.

rs3736228.description: LRP5 helps build bone density. This marker is strongly associated with lumbar spine bone density and osteoporosis risk.

variants.TT: Lower bone density / Higher fracture risk

rs3923809.description: The strongest genetic risk factor for Restless Legs Syndrome and periodic limb movements during sleep.

rs12913832.description: The primary determinant of Blue vs. Brown eyes (influencing OCA2).

rs6152.description: Determines the sensitivity of hair follicles to DHT. (Note: X-linked, so Men have one copy, Women have two).

rs1611115.description: DBH converts Dopamine into Norepinephrine (Adrenaline). Variants determine your balance of 'drive' (dopamine) vs 'alertness/anxiety' (adrenaline).

variants.TT: Low conversion (High Dopamine / Low Adrenaline - 'Cold hands', lower stress threshold)

rs4570625.description: TPH2 is the rate-limiting enzyme for creating serotonin in the brain. It strongly influences emotional stability and amygdala reactivity.

rs1360780.description: Regulates how quickly cortisol levels return to normal after stress. Risk variants are associated with prolonged stress responses and higher PTSD risk.

rs8007267.description: GCH1 produces BH4, a cofactor needed to make Dopamine and Serotonin. High producers often report lower chronic pain sensitivity.

rs4244285.description: Key enzyme for metabolizing many antidepressants (Lexapro, Celexa) and PPIs (Omeprazole). The 'A' allele indicates poor metabolism.

variants.AA: Poor metabolizer (Drugs may build up to toxic levels)

rs1045642.description: Controls the pump that pushes drugs/toxins out of the brain. Low activity means medications (like loperamide or antidepressants) may enter the brain more easily.

rs11558538.description: Unlike DAO (gut histamine), HNMT breaks down histamine inside cells (brain/liver). Critical for those with 'histamine intolerance' symptoms like insomnia/brain fog.

rs1001179.description: Catalase breaks down hydrogen peroxide. Low levels are associated with oxidative stress and earlier greying of hair.

rs1799930.description: NAT2 detoxifies carcinogens (like smoke/exhaust) and certain drugs. 'Slow acetylators' accumulate toxins faster and are more sensitive to chemicals.

variants.AA: Slow acetylator (Chemical sensitivity / higher risk from smoke)

rs17070145.description: Influences the hippocampus and memory retrieval. Carriers of the 'T' allele often perform better on memory tasks involving words and events.

rs1761667.description: CD36 is a receptor on the tongue that tastes fat. Low levels (A allele) make you less sensitive to the taste, often leading to consuming more fatty/creamy foods to get satisfaction.

rs73598374.description: ADA breaks down adenosine (the chemical that makes you sleepy). Slower breakdown leads to deeper, more intense slow-wave sleep and more grogginess upon waking.

variants.GA: Slower breakdown (Deeper sleeper / more sleep inertia)
variants.AG: Slower breakdown (Deeper sleeper / more sleep inertia)

rs20541.description: IL13 regulates IgE production (allergic response). Risk variants are strongly linked to hay fever, asthma, and general allergic sensitivity.

rs2016520.description: PPARD increases the burning of fat for fuel in muscles. The 'C' allele is the 'endurance' variant, enhancing long-distance performance.

variants.CC: High endurance potential (Elite endurance athlete trait)

rs1800407.description: Works with HERC2 to determine eye color. This specific marker helps distinguish between Brown and Green/Hazel eyes.

rs35874116.description: Determines how intensely you taste sugar. 'Low sensers' may add more sugar to coffee or tea to get the same level of sweetness.

variants.TT: Low sweet sensitivity (May consume more sugar)

rs72921001.description: Olfactory receptor that detects aldehydes in cilantro. For some, this gene makes cilantro taste like soap or dirt.

rs10427255.description: Determines if you sneeze when suddenly exposed to bright light (ACHOO syndrome).

rs13331.description: Determines whether you can smell the distinct odor in urine after eating asparagus. Everyone produces it, but only some can smell it.

rs1803274.description: Deficiency in this enzyme causes prolonged paralysis after using certain muscle relaxants (like succinylcholine) during surgery.

rs1051266.description: Controls the transport of folate into cells. Even with good MTHFR, poor transport can lead to low intracellular folate.

rs40184.description: Regulates how quickly dopamine is cleared from the synapse. High clearance means lower baseline dopamine; low clearance means higher dopamine.

variants.CC: High clearance (Lower baseline dopamine / Higher ADHD risk)

rs2066853.description: The Aryl Hydrocarbon Receptor regulates the body's response to environmental pollutants like dioxins and pesticides.

rs419598.description: IL1RN produces the 'off switch' for inflammation (IL-1 receptor antagonist). Low production leads to chronic, prolonged inflammation.

variants.TT: Low IL-1RA (Higher risk of chronic inflammation)

rs7454108.description: Complements the HLA-DQ2.5 marker. You can be negative for DQ2.5 but positive for DQ8 and still have a high risk for Celiac disease.

rs4588.description: Works with rs7041 (in your list) to define the Gc1s, Gc1f, and Gc2 isoforms. 'Gc2' carriers (A allele here) have lower Vitamin D affinity.

variants.AA: Gc2 variant (Lower affinity / lower D levels)

rs1801272.description: CYP2A6 breaks down nicotine. 'Slow metabolizers' feel the buzz longer and smoke fewer cigarettes, but may have harder withdrawals.

variants.TT: Fast metabolism (Smoke more to maintain levels)

rs553668.description: Influences how adrenaline suppresses insulin. Risk variants cause adrenaline to shut down insulin too strongly, leading to high blood sugar and belly fat.

variants.AK: Altered response (Note: K=G or T on some chips)

rs5370.description: EDN1 causes blood vessels to constrict. High levels are linked to higher blood pressure and vascular stiffness.

rs231775.description: CTLA4 acts as a brake on the immune system. Weaker 'brakes' (G allele) increase the risk of autoimmune thyroid issues (Hashimoto's/Graves).

rs7501331.description: Works alongside rs11645428. Having risk variants in BOTH significantly cripples the ability to turn carrots into Vitamin A.

variants.TT: Poor conversion (Need Retinol form of Vit A)

rs1946518.description: IL-18 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine linked to heart disease and metabolic syndrome.

rs1061170.description: CFH regulates inflammation in the eye. The 'T' (or H402) variant is a significant risk factor for macular degeneration as you age.

rs1052133.description: OGG1 repairs DNA damaged by oxidative stress (8-oxoG). The 'C' allele (Ser326Cys) has reduced activity, making cells more vulnerable to free radical damage.

variants.CC: Low repair capacity (Higher oxidative stress risk)

rs41423247.description: Influences how sensitive the body is to cortisol. The 'G' variant is linked to higher sensitivity, often resulting in higher belly fat and blood sugar under stress.

rs6746030.description: SCN9A controls sodium channels in pain neurons. The 'A' allele is associated with a lower threshold for pain (higher sensitivity).

rs12979860.description: Predicts the ability to clear certain viruses (like Hepatitis C) spontaneously. The 'C' allele indicates a robust interferon response.

rs2569190.description: CD14 detects bacteria in the gut. The 'T' allele leads to higher CD14 levels, causing stronger inflammation when gut barrier is permeable (leaky gut).

variants.CC: Lower inflammatory response to gut bacteria
variants.TT: High inflammatory response (Sensitive to gut issues)

rs1799998.description: Regulates aldosterone synthesis. High producers retain more sodium and water, raising blood pressure.

rs1800849.description: UCP3 uncouples mitochondria in muscle cells. High activity variants are linked to faster metabolic rate and fat burning in muscles.

rs6275.description: Influences the density and stability of dopamine receptors. The 'T' allele is associated with lower receptor density, affecting motivation and addiction risk.

rs671.description: The specific mutation responsible for the 'Asian Flush'. It causes a severe inability to break down acetaldehyde.

rs6025.description: This is the specific R506Q mutation for Factor V Leiden. It is the gold standard marker for hereditary clotting risk.

rs11209026.description: A rare variant where the 'A' allele provides significant protection against developing Crohn's disease and Ulcerative Colitis.

rs16906252.description: MMP12 breaks down elastin in the lungs. High activity variants increase the risk of COPD and asthma, especially in smokers.

variants.TT: Higher risk of lung elasticity loss

rs2943641.description: IRS1 is crucial for insulin signaling. The 'T' allele is associated with insulin resistance and higher fasting insulin levels.

rs35652124.description: NRF2 regulates the expression of hundreds of antioxidant and detox genes. Variants affect the body's overall ability to handle oxidative stress.

rs1800440.description: Converts estrogen into the potentially damaging 4-hydroxy form. High activity (Asn453Ser) increases oxidative stress from estrogen metabolites.

rs9536314.description: Klotho is a powerful anti-aging protein. The 'F' allele (related to this variant) is associated with better cognition and longer life span in heterozygotes.

rs521674.description: This gene influences how strongly the brain records emotional events. Carriers often have vivid memories and may be more susceptible to PTSD.

variants.GG: Strong emotional memory / Higher trauma sensitivity

rs1006737.description: A calcium channel gene linked to bipolar disorder, sleep quality, and creativity. It affects how excitable brain neurons are.

rs11568820.description: Influences the VDR in the intestine. The 'A' allele (sometimes reported as 'T' depending on strand) reduces Vitamin D absorption from food.

rs1143627.description: Alongside rs16944, this marker regulates IL-1 Beta production. High producers have more robust immune responses but higher chronic inflammation risk.

rs894160.description: PLIN1 coats fat droplets. Risk variants make it harder for the body to access stored fat for energy, leading to weight loss resistance.

variants.GG: Resistant to weight loss (Requires strict caloric deficit)

rs12778366.description: SIRT1 is the 'guardian of the genome.' This variant affects long-term metabolic health and protection against age-related diabetes.

rs3813867.description: CYP2E1 breaks down alcohol, acetaminophen, and industrial solvents. High activity generates more free radicals during detox.

variants.GC: Higher activity (More oxidative stress from alcohol)
variants.CG: Higher activity (More oxidative stress from alcohol)
variants.CC: High induction (Needs more antioxidants when drinking)

rs7294919.description: CNTNAP2 influences coordination, skill learning, and precision.
